nodes.label: AI as educational resource for background knowledge
nodes.label_negative: AI as unreliable for acute or emergency situations
nodes.grounding_span: AI is best used when I know I don't know and need the information or background

nodes.label: AI as granular search engine for concrete answers
nodes.label_negative: AI as conversational partner for advice
nodes.grounding_span: I use it as a granular search engine basically. I'm not looking for advice or conversation, I'm looking for answers that are concrete

nodes.label: AI as collaborative design partner
nodes.label_negative: AI as unresponsive to fine-tuning requests
nodes.grounding_span: In those cases I am collaborating with AI and trying to get projects or write-ups to be effective and high quality

nodes.label: AI as enhancer of paramedic skills and safety
nodes.label_negative: AI as threat to paramedic jobs
nodes.grounding_span: If anything AI will make paramedics way better

nodes.label: AI as shortcut used by younger colleagues
nodes.label_negative: AI as quality tool for genuine learning
nodes.grounding_span: In their case they seem to use it as a dodge or a shortcut

nodes.label: Patient care and safety
nodes.grounding_span: This will result in better patient outcomes

nodes.label: Professional competence and efficiency
nodes.grounding_span: AI rarely let's me down and the small complaints are so trivial compared to alternatives

nodes.label: Quality and effectiveness in work products
nodes.grounding_span: trying to get projects or write-ups to be effective and high quality

nodes.label: Satisfaction with AI as reliable educational tool
nodes.grounding_span: It's a great resource for education and information

nodes.label: Frustration with AI's lack of responsiveness to fine-tuning
nodes.grounding_span: It was frustrating to some extent putting together the survey, because I had trouble fine tuning the design

nodes.label: Optimism about AI's future impact on paramedicine
nodes.grounding_span: I think AI will also have the potential to help train and education EMS providers to a much higher standard

nodes.label: Critical view of younger colleagues' AI use as shortcut
nodes.grounding_span: I don't see a lot of quality in terms of how they are using AI. In their case they seem to use it as a dodge or a shortcut

nodes.label: Context-dependent tool switching — adapts interaction style to task domain
nodes.grounding_span: When I use AI in the back of an ambulance I use it as a granular search engine basically... In those cases I am collaborating with AI

nodes.label: Explicit boundary setting between emergency and non-emergency situations
nodes.grounding_span: Anything EMS related or acute changes in the patient would default to me doing what I know
